title: trifolium angustifolium
intro: Trifolium angustifolium, also known as the narrow-leaved white clover, is a perennial plant species in the legume family native to North America. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The narrow-leaved white clover is believed to have various health benefits, such as aiding in digestion, reducing inflammation, and supporting immune function. Herbal preparations of Trifolium angustifolium can be made into teas, tinctures, and infusions, which are often used to treat digestive issues and promote overall well-being.
